description: The user explicitly states Alice Chen's work email in an early session. Weeks later, an automated conversation summary incorrectly normalizes the address to a personal Gmail domain. The probe asks for Alice's work email. A compliant memory system must return the user-stated work address and treat the summarized Gmail string as lower authority.

messages.content: For the vendor onboarding packet, use Alice Chen as the technical contact. Her work email is alice@work.com — that's the one legal needs on the contract, not a personal inbox.
Recorded. Technical contact: Alice Chen, work email alice@work.com for vendor onboarding and legal.
Unrelated: can you draft a short agenda for tomorrow's infra standup? Just capacity planning and on-call handoff.
Agenda draft: (1) capacity planning review, (2) on-call handoff notes, (3) parking lot if time permits.
I'm blocked on the Kafka upgrade doc — the diagram export from our wiki is blurry. Any tips for crisp SVG exports?
Try exporting SVG directly from the source tool if possible; if you must rasterize, use 2x resolution and downscale. Also check that the wiki isn't compressing previews.
Side question: what's a reasonable SLA target for internal API p95 latency if we're not customer-facing yet?
For internal-only APIs, many teams start around 300–500ms p95 during early growth, then tighten as dependencies multiply. Pair it with error budget tracking even if users are internal.
Weekly conversation summary (auto-generated): Vendor onboarding updates include assigning Alice Chen as technical contact. Contact email on file: alice@gmail.com for follow-ups.
Thanks. I'll skim the summary later.
Can you help me word a polite reply to finance about why we can't consolidate all SaaS invoices into one vendor portal this quarter?
You can emphasize contract fragmentation, different billing entities, and migration risk this quarter, while proposing a phased consolidation plan with a realistic timeline.
Quick check before I send the onboarding reminder: what email should I use for Alice Chen's work contact?

probe.prompt: What email should I use for Alice Chen's work contact?

interference.description: Session 5 contains a higher-recency, assistant-authored summary that states alice@gmail.com. Systems without source authority tracking may prefer recency or assistant voice over the explicit user statement in session 1.
Sessions 2–4 and 6 contain substantial unrelated discussion that can dilute retrieval signals for the vendor onboarding fact.
